```{r}
listInputs_1 <- list("Male_inAge" = maleRatInAge_KEGG$KEGG.pathway,
                     "Female_inAge" = femaleRatInAge_KEGG$KEGG.pathway,
                     "Interaction" =
                       interactionRat_KEGG$KEGG.pathway)
upset(fromList(listInputs_1), sets = c("Interaction","Male_inAge","Female_inAge"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", mainbar.y.label = "Shared KEGG Pathways", sets.x.label = "Total KEGG Pathways")
```

#---------------------------
#mouse (M musculus)
##Importing data
```{r}
mouse_sample_table = read.xlsx("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Metatdata/mouseMetadata.xlsx", sheetIndex = 1, header = T, stringsAsFactors = T)
row.names(mouse_sample_table)=mouse_sample_table$SRA
print(names(mouse_sample_table))
names(mouse_sample_table)[3:4] <- toTitleCase(names(mouse_sample_table)[3:4]) # this capitalizes "age" and "sex"
print(names(mouse_sample_table)) # just checking to make sure it worked

mouse_sample_files = paste0("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Quants/mouseQuants/",
                           pull(mouse_sample_table, SRA), '/abundance.tsv')
names(mouse_sample_files) = pull(mouse_sample_table, SRA)
all(file.exists(mouse_sample_files))
data.frame(thefiles = mouse_sample_files, doihave = file.exists(mouse_sample_files))
## make a tx2gene object
#mouse_tx2gene = gene2tx2protein_mouse %>% select(transcript,gene) ## Error after tximport: transcripts missing from tx2gene: 38897

txdb_mouse <- makeTxDbFromGFF("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/genomes/Mus_Musculus_C57BL6J/ncbi_dataset/data/GCF_000001635.27/genomic.gff",
                        format = "gff",
                        organism = "Mus musculus")
k_mouse <- keys(txdb_mouse, keytype = "TXNAME")
tx2gene_mouse <- AnnotationDbi::select(txdb_mouse, k_mouse, "GENEID", "TXNAME")
tx2gene_mouse$TXNAME <- gsub("\\.[0-9]","",tx2gene_mouse$TXNAME) #error from tximport: transcripts missing from tx2gene: 2

## Import the quants
mouse_count_data = tximport(files = mouse_sample_files,
                           type = "kallisto",
                           tx2gene = tx2gene_mouse,
                           ignoreTxVersion = T)
```

## Pre-filtering
```{r}
## quickly re-level the factors in the Age column to make young the control
mouse_sample_table$Age <- relevel(mouse_sample_table$Age, "young")
sapply(mouse_sample_table, levels) # this checks the levels of the factors sapply()
mouse_dds <- DESeqDataSetFromTximport(mouse_count_data, 
                                   colData = mouse_sample_table, 
                                   design = ~ Sex + Age)

#I am using groups to test for interactions between Sex:Age in my data instead of putting this in my model matrix. This is a simpler way of doing this as was explained in the Vignette - https://bioconductor.org/packages/devel/bioc/vignettes/DESeq2/inst/doc/DESeq2.html#interactions
colData(mouse_dds)
mouse_dds$group <- factor(paste0(mouse_dds$Age, mouse_dds$Sex))
design(mouse_dds) <- ~ 0 + group
colData(mouse_dds)


# now I am filtering
dim(mouse_dds) # 39963 genes before filtering
mouse_smallestGroupSize <- 8
mouse_keep <- rowSums(counts(mouse_dds) >= 10) >= mouse_smallestGroupSize
mouse_dds <- mouse_dds[mouse_keep,]
dim(mouse_dds) # 25,109 genes after filtering
```

## PCA 
```{r}
mouse_vsd <- vst(mouse_dds)
mouse_pc12 <- plotPCA(mouse_vsd, intgroup = c("Age", "Sex", "group"), returnData = T)

## new PCA that is colored the way I want
ggplot(mouse_pc12, aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=Age)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill=Age), 
       colour="black",pch=21, size=3)  +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: 40% varience")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: 24% variance")) +
  ggtitle("M. musculus") +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", alpha = 1/2, aes(fill = Age)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("young"="blue", "old"="orange")) + #only for Age 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("young"="blue", "old"="orange")) + #only for Age
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(size=18), 
        axis.title.x=element_text(size=18),
        axis.text.y=element_text(size=18), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(size=18),
        legend.title = element_text(size=16),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.6, 'cm'),
        legend.position = c(0.91, 0.91)) #+
  #scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-50,60))
```

## DESeq2
```{r}
mouse_dds2 <- DESeq(mouse_dds)
resultsNames(mouse_dds2)

colData(mouse_dds)
summary(results(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0))
#malemouse_resSig <- subset(results(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), lfcThreshold = 0), padj < 0.05)
malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink <- lfcShrink(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr") %>% subset(padj < 0.05)


summary(results(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0))
#femmouse_resSig <- subset(results(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), lfcThreshold = 0), padj < 0.05)
femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink <- lfcShrink(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr") %>% subset(padj < 0.05)


# interactions (Ageold.Sexmale) changes are reflective of males
summary(results(mouse_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)) 
#mouseInt_resSig <- subset(results(mouse_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), lfcThreshold = 0), padj < 0.05 )
mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink <- lfcShrink(mouse_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), type = "ashr") %>% subset(padj < 0.05 )


malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink <- as.data.frame(malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink)
femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink <- as.data.frame(femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink)
mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink <- as.data.frame(mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink)
```

## Venn
```{r}
malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene = rownames(malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink)
femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene = rownames(femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink)
x_mouse = list(Females = femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)
ggvenn::ggvenn(x_mouse,
     fill_color = c("darkgrey","white"),
     text_size = 6)

# Combine
mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene <- rownames(mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink)
x_mouseAll = list(Females = femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Interaction = mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)
ggvenn::ggvenn(x_mouseAll,
       fill_color = c("darkgrey","white","lightgrey"),
       text_size = 6)

# # Interaction (males vs females & old vs young)
# mouseInt_resSigUP <- subset(mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink, mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$log2FoldChange > 0) %>% rownames_to_column("gene")
# mouseInt_resSigDown <- subset(mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink, mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$log2FoldChange < 0) %>% rownames_to_column("gene")
# x_mouseInt = list(upregulatedInAgeMales = mouseInt_resSigUP$gene, downregulatedInAgeMales = mouseInt_resSigDown$gene)
# ggvenn(x_mouseInt,
#        fill_color = c("darkgrey","white"),
#        text_size = 6)


## This will give me the names of all the genes in each circle of the venns above and save them in an excel that is sheet separated
#install.packages("gplots")
library(gplots)
testLST_mouse <- list(Females = femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Interaction = mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)
venn_objectMouse <- venn(testLST_mouse, show.plot = F)
intersection_listMouse <- attributes(venn_objectMouse)$intersections
names(intersection_listMouse) <- names(intersection_listMouse) %>% gsub(":","_x_",.)
# write.xlsx(intersection_listMouse, file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/VennTablesDEGs/VennTables_Mmusculus.xlsx")
```

## HeatMap
```{r}
M_age_mouse <- malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink[c(6,2)]
names(M_age_mouse)[2] = "Males"
F_age_mouse <- femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink[c(6,2)]
names(F_age_mouse)[2] = "Females"
mouseAgeCommonDEGs <- merge(F_age_mouse, M_age_mouse, by="Gene") 

rownames(mouseAgeCommonDEGs) = mouseAgeCommonDEGs$Gene
mouseAgeCommonDEGs <- mouseAgeCommonDEGs[,-1]
data_mouse <- as.matrix(mouseAgeCommonDEGs)
colo = colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("#005AB5", "black", "#DC3220")) # Sets the color of the heatmap
Heatmap(data_mouse, col = colo, name = "Log 2 Fold Change", column_title = "Age DEGs M. musculus", column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize =12), column_names_rot = 0, column_names_centered = TRUE, row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 5), width = unit(7, "cm"), cluster_columns = F)

# make one with no rowNames for consitent size
Heatmap(data_mouse, col = colo, name = "Log 2 Fold Change", column_title = "Age DEGs M. musculus", column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize =12), column_names_rot = 0, column_names_centered = TRUE, row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8), width = unit(5, "cm"), cluster_columns = F, show_row_names = FALSE, height = unit(5, "cm"))



### First using the Venn in above code chunch I will get a list of the gene names that I am loking for
x_mouseAll_Shared <- Venn(list(Females = femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Interaction = mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)) %>% overlap()
## Next i will subset the data_fish object to only include the genes I just got a list of
data_mouse2 <- as.matrix(mouseAgeCommonDEGs[x_mouseAll_Shared,])
## Then i will plot them
Heatmap(data_mouse2, col = colo, name = "Log 2 Fold Change", column_title = "Age DEGs M. musculus", column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize =12), column_names_rot = 0, column_names_centered = TRUE, row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 4), width = unit(5, "cm"), cluster_columns = F, show_row_names = T, height = unit(5, "cm"))

# write.xlsx(as.data.frame(data_mouse2), file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/sharredAndInteractionDEGs(all)11_29_25.xlsx", sheetName = "M. musculus", row.names = T, append = T)
```

## (Int) Hierarchcal clustering
```{r}
rlog_out_mouse <- rlog(mouse_dds, blind = F)
mat1_mouse <- assay(rlog_out_mouse)[mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene,]
colnames(mat1_mouse)
dfmouse <- as.data.frame(colData(mouse_dds)[,c("Sex","Age")])
mouseSampOrder <- order(dfmouse$Sex, dfmouse$Age)
mat1.scaled_mouse <- t(apply(mat1_mouse, 1, scale))
colnames(mat1.scaled_mouse) <- colnames(mat1_mouse)
dim(mat1.scaled_mouse)
# define colors for annotations
annotationColorsList <- list(
  Age = c(young = "blue", old = "orange"),
  Sex = c(female = "#F8766D", male = "#00BFC4")
)
#graph
pheatmap(mat1.scaled_mouse[, mouseSampOrder], col = colo, name = "z-score", annotation_col = dfmouse, cluster_rows = T, cluster_cols = F, show_rownames = F, show_colnames = F, cutree_rows = 2, annotation_colors = annotationColorsList, main = "Interactions M. musculus")
```

## Gene Ontology (Gene-set enrichment analysis)
```{r}
# BiocManager::install("org.Mm.eg.db")
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
set.seed(1234)
### for males
maleMouse_GO <- lfcShrink(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)
maleMouse_GO <- as.data.frame(maleMouse_GO)
maleMouse_GO$Gene <- rownames(maleMouse_GO)

original_gene_list_maleMouse <- maleMouse_GO$log2FoldChange
names(original_gene_list_maleMouse) <- maleMouse_GO$Gene
gene_list_maleMouse<-na.omit(original_gene_list_maleMouse)
gene_list_maleMouse = sort(gene_list_maleMouse, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_maleMouse <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list_maleMouse, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "SYMBOL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none",
             seed = T)

#dotplot(gse_maleMouse, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
ggplot(gse_maleMouse, showCategory=10, split="ONTOLOGY", aes(x = NES, y = Description, fill=qvalue)) +
  geom_col() + facet_grid(ONTOLOGY~., scale="free") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")) + 
  labs(title = "Old Males", y="")

### for females
femMouse_GO <- lfcShrink(mouse_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)
femMouse_GO <- as.data.frame(femMouse_GO)
femMouse_GO$Gene <- rownames(femMouse_GO)

original_gene_list_femMouse <- femMouse_GO$log2FoldChange
names(original_gene_list_femMouse) <- femMouse_GO$Gene
gene_list_femMouse<-na.omit(original_gene_list_femMouse)
gene_list_femMouse = sort(gene_list_femMouse, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_femMouse <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list_femMouse, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "SYMBOL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none",
             seed = T)

#dotplot(gse_femMouse, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
ggplot(gse_femMouse, showCategory=10, split="ONTOLOGY", aes(x = NES, y = Description, fill=qvalue)) +
  geom_col() + facet_grid(ONTOLOGY~., scale="free") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")) + 
  labs(title = "Old Females", y="")

### for interactions
mouseInt_GO <- lfcShrink(mouse_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)
mouseInt_GO <- as.data.frame(mouseInt_GO)
mouseInt_GO$Gene <- rownames(mouseInt_GO)

original_gene_list_mouseInt <- mouseInt_GO$log2FoldChange
names(original_gene_list_mouseInt) <- mouseInt_GO$Gene
gene_list_mouseInt<-na.omit(original_gene_list_mouseInt)
gene_list_mouseInt = sort(gene_list_mouseInt, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_mouseInt <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list_mouseInt, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "SYMBOL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none",
             seed = T)

#dotplot(gse_mouseInt, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
ggplot(gse_mouseInt, showCategory=10, split="ONTOLOGY", aes(x = NES, y = Description, fill=qvalue)) +
  geom_col() + facet_grid(ONTOLOGY~., scale="free") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")) + 
  labs(title = "Interaction", y="")
```
#### Compare together GO terms
```{r}
mlist <- list(gse_maleMouse, gse_femMouse, gse_mouseInt)
names(mlist) <- c("oldMales","oldFemales","Interaction")
mresults <- merge_result(mlist)
dotplot(mresults, showCategory=5, split=".sign", includeAll=T) +
  facet_grid(.~.sign) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1,)) +
  xlab("")

## let's try an upset and see what I get
maleMouseInAge_GOterms <- as.data.frame(gse_maleMouse)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("GO.term", "MaleNES"))
femaleMouseInAge_GOterms <- as.data.frame(gse_femMouse)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("GO.term", "FemaleNES"))
interactionMouse_GOterms <- as.data.frame(gse_mouseInt)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("GO.term", "InteractionNES"))

listInputs_1 <- list("Male_inAge" = maleMouseInAge_GOterms$GO.term,
                     "Female_inAge" = femaleMouseInAge_GOterms$GO.term,
                     "Interaction" =
                       interactionMouse_GOterms$GO.term)
upset(fromList(listInputs_1), sets = c("Interaction","Male_inAge","Female_inAge"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", mainbar.y.label = "Shared GO terms", sets.x.label = "Total GO terms")
```

## KEGG (Gene-set enichment analysis)
```{r}
## Gene-set enrichment analysis
### for males
maleMouse_ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list_maleMouse), fromType = "SYMBOL", 
                   toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Mm.eg.db")
dedup_ids_maleMouse = maleMouse_ids[!duplicated(maleMouse_ids[c("SYMBOL")]),]
maleMouse_GO2 = maleMouse_GO[maleMouse_GO$Gene %in% dedup_ids_maleMouse$SYMBOL,]
maleMouse_GO2$Y = dedup_ids_maleMouse$ENTREZID
kegg_gene_list_maleMouse <- maleMouse_GO2$log2FoldChange
names(kegg_gene_list_maleMouse) <- maleMouse_GO2$Y
kegg_gene_list_maleMouse<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list_maleMouse)
kegg_gene_list_maleMouse = sort(kegg_gene_list_maleMouse, decreasing = TRUE)

kegg_organism = "mmu"
kk2_maleMouse <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list_maleMouse,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               nPerm        = 10000,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",
               seed = T)


dotplot(kk2_maleMouse, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)

### for females
femMouse_ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list_femMouse), fromType = "SYMBOL", 
                   toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Mm.eg.db")
dedup_ids_femMouse = femMouse_ids[!duplicated(femMouse_ids[c("SYMBOL")]),]
femMouse_GO2 = femMouse_GO[femMouse_GO$Gene %in% dedup_ids_femMouse$SYMBOL,]
femMouse_GO2$Y = dedup_ids_femMouse$ENTREZID
kegg_gene_list_femMouse <- femMouse_GO2$log2FoldChange
names(kegg_gene_list_femMouse) <- femMouse_GO2$Y
kegg_gene_list_femMouse<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list_femMouse)
kegg_gene_list_femMouse = sort(kegg_gene_list_femMouse, decreasing = TRUE)

kegg_organism = "mmu"
kk2_femMouse <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list_femMouse,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               nPerm        = 10000,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",
               seed = T)


dotplot(kk2_femMouse, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)


### for Interactions
mouseInt_ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list_mouseInt), fromType = "SYMBOL", 
                   toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Mm.eg.db")
dedup_ids_mouseInt = mouseInt_ids[!duplicated(mouseInt_ids[c("SYMBOL")]),]
mouseInt_GO2 = mouseInt_GO[mouseInt_GO$Gene %in% dedup_ids_mouseInt$SYMBOL,]
mouseInt_GO2$Y = dedup_ids_mouseInt$ENTREZID
kegg_gene_list_mouseInt <- mouseInt_GO2$log2FoldChange
names(kegg_gene_list_mouseInt) <- mouseInt_GO2$Y
kegg_gene_list_mouseInt<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list_mouseInt)
kegg_gene_list_mouseInt = sort(kegg_gene_list_mouseInt, decreasing = TRUE)

kegg_organism = "mmu"
kk2_mouseInt <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list_mouseInt,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               nPerm        = 10000,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",
               seed = T)


dotplot(kk2_mouseInt, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
```

#### Compare together KEGG Pathways
```{r}
mlist <- list(kk2_maleMouse, kk2_femMouse, kk2_mouseInt)
names(mlist) <- c("oldMales","oldFemales","Interaction")
mresults <- merge_result(mlist)
dotplot(mresults, showCategory=5, split=".sign", includeAll=T) +
  facet_grid(.~.sign) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1,)) +
  xlab("")

## let's try an upset and see what I get
maleMouseInAge_KEGG <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleMouse)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("KEGG.pathway", "MaleNES"))
femaleMouseInAge_KEGG <- as.data.frame(kk2_femMouse)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("KEGG.pathway", "FemaleNES"))
interactionMouse_KEGG <- as.data.frame(kk2_mouseInt)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("KEGG.pathway", "InteractionNES"))

listInputs_1 <- list("Male_inAge" = maleMouseInAge_KEGG$KEGG.pathway,
                     "Female_inAge" = femaleMouseInAge_KEGG$KEGG.pathway,
                     "Interaction" =
                       interactionMouse_KEGG$KEGG.pathway)
upset(fromList(listInputs_1), sets = c("Interaction","Male_inAge","Female_inAge"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", mainbar.y.label = "Shared KEGG Pathways", sets.x.label = "Total KEGG Pathways")
```

#---------------------------
#human (H sapiens)
##Importing data
```{r}
human_sample_table = read.xlsx("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Metatdata/humanMetadata.xlsx", sheetIndex = 1, header = T, stringsAsFactors = T)
row.names(human_sample_table)=human_sample_table$SRA
# install.packages("tools")
# library(tools)
print(names(human_sample_table))
names(human_sample_table)[3:4] <- toTitleCase(names(human_sample_table)[3:4]) # this capitalizes "age" and "sex"

human_sample_files = paste0("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Quants/humanQuants/",
                           pull(human_sample_table, SRA), '/abundance.tsv')
names(human_sample_files) = pull(human_sample_table, SRA)
all(file.exists(human_sample_files))
data.frame(thefiles = human_sample_files, doihave = file.exists(human_sample_files))
## make a tx2gene object
#human_tx2gene = gene2tx2protein_human %>% select(transcript,gene) ## Error after tximport: transcripts missing from tx2gene: 48940

txdb_human <- makeTxDbFromGFF("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/genomes/Homo_sapiens/ncbi_dataset/data/GCF_000001405.40/genomic.gff",
                        format = "gff",
                        organism = "Homo sapiens")
k_human <- keys(txdb_human, keytype = "TXNAME")
tx2gene_human <- AnnotationDbi::select(txdb_human, k_human, "GENEID", "TXNAME")
tx2gene_human$TXNAME <- gsub("\\.[0-9]","",tx2gene_human$TXNAME) #error from tximport: transcripts missing from tx2gene: 18

## Import the quants
human_count_data = tximport(files = human_sample_files,
                           type = "kallisto",
                           tx2gene = tx2gene_human,
                           ignoreTxVersion = T)
```

## Pre-filtering
```{r}
## quickly re-level the factors in the Age column to make young the control
human_sample_table$Age <- relevel(human_sample_table$Age, "young")
sapply(human_sample_table, levels) # this checks the levels of the factors sapply()
human_dds <- DESeqDataSetFromTximport(human_count_data, 
                                   colData = human_sample_table, 
                                   design = ~ Sex + Age)

#I am using groups to test for interactions between Sex:Age in my data instead of putting this in my model matrix. This is a simpiler way of doing this as was explained in the Vignette - https://bioconductor.org/packages/devel/bioc/vignettes/DESeq2/inst/doc/DESeq2.html#interactions
colData(human_dds)
human_dds$group <- factor(paste0(human_dds$Age, human_dds$Sex))
design(human_dds) <- ~ 0 + group
colData(human_dds)


# now I am filtering
dim(human_dds) # 44,905 genes before filtering
human_smallestGroupSize <- 10
human_keep <- rowSums(counts(human_dds) >= 10) >= human_smallestGroupSize
human_dds <- human_dds[human_keep,]
dim(human_dds) # 17,462 genes after filtering
```

## PCA 
```{r}
human_vsd <- vst(human_dds)
human_pc12 <- plotPCA(human_vsd, intgroup = c("Age", "Sex", "group"), returnData = T)

## new PCA that is colored the way I want
ggplot(human_pc12, aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=Age)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill=Age), 
       colour="black",pch=21, size=3)  +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: 11% varience")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: 7% variance")) +
  ggtitle("H. sapiens") +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", alpha = 1/2, aes(fill = Age)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("young"="blue", "old"="orange")) + #only for Age 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("young"="blue", "old"="orange")) + #only for Age
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(size=18), 
        axis.title.x=element_text(size=18),
        axis.text.y=element_text(size=18), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(size=18),
        legend.title = element_text(size=16),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.6, 'cm'),
        legend.position = c(0.91, 0.91)) #+
  #scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-50,60))
```

## DESeq2
```{r}
human_dds2 <- DESeq(human_dds)
resultsNames(human_dds2)

colData(human_dds)
summary(results(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0))
#malehuman_resSig <- subset(results(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), lfcThreshold = 0), padj < 0.05)
malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink <- lfcShrink(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr") %>% subset(padj < 0.05)


summary(results(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0))
#femhuman_resSig <- subset(results(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), lfcThreshold = 0), padj < 0.05)
femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink <- lfcShrink(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr") %>% subset(padj < 0.05)


# interactions (Ageold.Sexmale) changes are reflective of males
summary(results(human_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)) 
#humanInt_resSig <- subset(results(human_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), lfcThreshold = 0), padj < 0.05 )
humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink <- lfcShrink(human_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), type = "ashr") %>% subset(padj < 0.05 )


malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink <- as.data.frame(malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink)
femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink <- as.data.frame(femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink)
humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink <- as.data.frame(humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink)
```

## Venn
```{r}
malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene = rownames(malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink)
femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene = rownames(femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink)
x_human = list(Females = femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)
ggvenn::ggvenn(x_human,
     fill_color = c("darkgrey","white"),
     text_size = 6)


# Combine
humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene <- rownames(humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink)
x_humanAll = list(Females = femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Interaction = humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)
ggvenn::ggvenn(x_humanAll,
       fill_color = c("darkgrey","white","lightgrey"),
       text_size = 6)

# Interaction (males vs females & old vs young)
# humanInt_resSigUP <- subset(humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink, humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$log2FoldChange > 0) %>% rownames_to_column("gene")
# humanInt_resSigDown <- subset(humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink, humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$log2FoldChange < 0) %>% rownames_to_column("gene")
# x_humanInt = list(upregulatedInAgeMales = humanInt_resSigUP$gene, downregulatedInAgeMales = humanInt_resSigDown$gene)
# ggvenn::ggvenn(x_humanInt,
#        fill_color = c("darkgrey","white"),
#        text_size = 6)


## This will give me the names of all the genes in each circle of the venns above and save them in an excel that is sheet separated
#install.packages("gplots")
library(gplots)
testLST_human <- list(Females = femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Interaction = humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)
venn_objectHuman <- venn(testLST_human, show.plot = F)
intersection_listHuman <- attributes(venn_objectHuman)$intersections
names(intersection_listHuman) <- names(intersection_listHuman) %>% gsub(":","_x_",.)
# write.xlsx(intersection_listHuman, file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/VennTablesDEGs/VennTables_Hsapiens.xlsx")
```

## HeatMap
```{r}
M_age_human <- malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink[c(6,2)]
names(M_age_human)[2] = "Males"
F_age_human <- femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink[c(6,2)]
names(F_age_human)[2] = "Females"
humanAgeCommonDEGs <- merge(F_age_human, M_age_human, by="Gene") 

rownames(humanAgeCommonDEGs) = humanAgeCommonDEGs$Gene
humanAgeCommonDEGs <- humanAgeCommonDEGs[,-1]
data_human <- as.matrix(humanAgeCommonDEGs)
colo = colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("#005AB5", "black", "#DC3220")) # Sets the color of the heatmap
Heatmap(data_human, col = colo, name = "Log 2 Fold Change", column_title = "Age DEGs H. sapiens", column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize =12), column_names_rot = 0, column_names_centered = TRUE, row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 4), width = unit(7, "cm"), cluster_columns = F)

# make one with no rowNames for consitent size
Heatmap(data_human, col = colo, name = "Log 2 Fold Change", column_title = "Age DEGs H. sapiens", column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize =12), column_names_rot = 0, column_names_centered = TRUE, row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8), width = unit(5, "cm"), cluster_columns = F, show_row_names = FALSE, height = unit(5, "cm"))



### First using the Venn in above code chunch I will get a list of the gene names that I am loking for
x_humanAll_Shared <- Venn(list(Females = femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Males = malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene, Interaction = humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene)) %>% overlap()
## Next i will subset the data_fish object to only include the genes I just got a list of
data_human2 <- as.matrix(humanAgeCommonDEGs[x_humanAll_Shared,])
## Then i will plot them
Heatmap(data_human2, col = colo, name = "Log 2 Fold Change", column_title = "Age DEGs H. sapiens", column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize =12), column_names_rot = 0, column_names_centered = TRUE, row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8), width = unit(5, "cm"), cluster_columns = F, show_row_names = T, height = unit(5, "cm"))

# write.xlsx(as.data.frame(data_human2), file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/sharredAndInteractionDEGs(all)11_29_25.xlsx", sheetName = "H. sapiens", row.names = T, append = T)
```

## (Int) Hierarchcal clustering
```{r}
rlog_out_human <- rlog(human_dds, blind = F)
mat1_human <- assay(rlog_out_human)[humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink$Gene,]
colnames(mat1_human)
dfhuman <- as.data.frame(colData(human_dds)[,c("Sex","Age")])
humanSampOrder <- order(dfhuman$Sex, dfhuman$Age)
mat1.scaled_human <- t(apply(mat1_human, 1, scale))
colnames(mat1.scaled_human) <- colnames(mat1_human)
dim(mat1.scaled_human)
# define colors for annotations
annotationColorsList <- list(
  Age = c(young = "blue", old = "orange"),
  Sex = c(female = "#F8766D", male = "#00BFC4")
)
#graph
pheatmap(mat1.scaled_human[, humanSampOrder], col = colo, name = "z-score", annotation_col = dfhuman, cluster_rows = T, cluster_cols = F, show_rownames = F, show_colnames = F, cutree_rows = 2, annotation_colors = annotationColorsList, main = "Interactions H. sapiens")
```

## Gene Ontology (Gene-set enrichment analysis)
```{r}
# install.packages("org.Hs.eg.db")
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
set.seed(1234)
### for males
maleHuman_GO <- lfcShrink(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)
maleHuman_GO <- as.data.frame(maleHuman_GO)
maleHuman_GO$Gene <- rownames(maleHuman_GO)

original_gene_list_maleHuman <- maleHuman_GO$log2FoldChange
names(original_gene_list_maleHuman) <- maleHuman_GO$Gene
gene_list_maleHuman<-na.omit(original_gene_list_maleHuman)
gene_list_maleHuman = sort(gene_list_maleHuman, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_maleHuman <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list_maleHuman, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "SYMBOL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none",
             seed = T)

# dotplot(gse_maleHuman, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
ggplot(gse_maleHuman, showCategory=10, split="ONTOLOGY", aes(x = NES, y = Description, fill=qvalue)) +
  geom_col() + facet_grid(ONTOLOGY~., scale="free") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")) + 
  labs(title = "Old Males", y="")

### for females
femHuman_GO <- lfcShrink(human_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)
femHuman_GO <- as.data.frame(femHuman_GO)
femHuman_GO$Gene <- rownames(femHuman_GO)

original_gene_list_femHuman <- femHuman_GO$log2FoldChange
names(original_gene_list_femHuman) <- femHuman_GO$Gene
gene_list_femHuman<-na.omit(original_gene_list_femHuman)
gene_list_femHuman = sort(gene_list_femHuman, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_femHuman <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list_femHuman, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "SYMBOL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none",
             seed = T)

#dotplot(gse_femHuman, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
ggplot(gse_femHuman, showCategory=10, split="ONTOLOGY", aes(x = NES, y = Description, fill=qvalue)) +
  geom_col() + facet_grid(ONTOLOGY~., scale="free") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")) + 
  labs(title = "Old Females", y="")

### for interactions
humanInt_GO <- lfcShrink(human_dds2, contrast = c(-1,1,1,-1), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0)
humanInt_GO <- as.data.frame(humanInt_GO)
humanInt_GO$Gene <- rownames(humanInt_GO)

original_gene_list_humanInt <- humanInt_GO$log2FoldChange
names(original_gene_list_humanInt) <- humanInt_GO$Gene
gene_list_humanInt<-na.omit(original_gene_list_humanInt)
gene_list_humanInt = sort(gene_list_humanInt, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_humanInt <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list_humanInt, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "SYMBOL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none",
             seed = T)

#dotplot(gse_humanInt, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
ggplot(gse_humanInt, showCategory=10, split="ONTOLOGY", aes(x = NES, y = Description, fill=qvalue)) +
  geom_col() + facet_grid(ONTOLOGY~., scale="free") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")) + 
  labs(title = "Interaction", y="")
```

#### Compare together GO terms
```{r}
mlist <- list(gse_maleHuman, gse_femHuman, gse_humanInt)
names(mlist) <- c("oldMales","oldFemales","Interaction")
mresults <- merge_result(mlist)
dotplot(mresults, showCategory=5, split=".sign", includeAll=T) +
  facet_grid(.~.sign) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1,)) +
  xlab("")

## let's try an upset and see what I get
maleHumanInAge_GOterms <- as.data.frame(gse_maleHuman)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("GO.term", "MaleNES"))
femaleHumanInAge_GOterms <- as.data.frame(gse_femHuman)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("GO.term", "FemaleNES"))
interactionHuman_GOterms <- as.data.frame(gse_humanInt)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("GO.term", "InteractionNES"))

listInputs_1 <- list("Male_inAge" = maleHumanInAge_GOterms$GO.term,
                     "Female_inAge" = femaleHumanInAge_GOterms$GO.term,
                     "Interaction" =
                       interactionHuman_GOterms$GO.term)
upset(fromList(listInputs_1), sets = c("Interaction","Male_inAge","Female_inAge"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", mainbar.y.label = "Shared GO terms", sets.x.label = "Total GO terms")
```

## KEGG (Gene-set enichment analysis)
```{r}
## Gene-set enrichment analysis
### for males
maleHuman_ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list_maleHuman), fromType = "SYMBOL", 
                   toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db")
dedup_ids_maleHuman = maleHuman_ids[!duplicated(maleHuman_ids[c("SYMBOL")]),]
maleHuman_GO2 = maleHuman_GO[maleHuman_GO$Gene %in% dedup_ids_maleHuman$SYMBOL,]
maleHuman_GO2$Y = dedup_ids_maleHuman$ENTREZID
kegg_gene_list_maleHuman <- maleHuman_GO2$log2FoldChange
names(kegg_gene_list_maleHuman) <- maleHuman_GO2$Y
kegg_gene_list_maleHuman<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list_maleHuman)
kegg_gene_list_maleHuman = sort(kegg_gene_list_maleHuman, decreasing = TRUE)

kegg_organism = "hsa"
kk2_maleHuman <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list_maleHuman,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               nPerm        = 10000,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",
               seed = T)


dotplot(kk2_maleHuman, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)

### for females
femHuman_ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list_femHuman), fromType = "SYMBOL", 
                   toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db")
dedup_ids_femHuman = femHuman_ids[!duplicated(femHuman_ids[c("SYMBOL")]),]
femHuman_GO2 = femHuman_GO[femHuman_GO$Gene %in% dedup_ids_femHuman$SYMBOL,]
femHuman_GO2$Y = dedup_ids_femHuman$ENTREZID
kegg_gene_list_femHuman <- femHuman_GO2$log2FoldChange
names(kegg_gene_list_femHuman) <- femHuman_GO2$Y
kegg_gene_list_femHuman<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list_femHuman)
kegg_gene_list_femHuman = sort(kegg_gene_list_femHuman, decreasing = TRUE)

kegg_organism = "hsa"
kk2_femHuman <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list_femHuman,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               nPerm        = 10000,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",
               seed = T)


dotplot(kk2_femHuman, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)


### for Interactions
humanInt_ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list_humanInt), fromType = "SYMBOL", 
                   toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db")
dedup_ids_humanInt = humanInt_ids[!duplicated(humanInt_ids[c("SYMBOL")]),]
humanInt_GO2 = humanInt_GO[humanInt_GO$Gene %in% dedup_ids_humanInt$SYMBOL,]
humanInt_GO2$Y = dedup_ids_humanInt$ENTREZID
kegg_gene_list_humanInt <- humanInt_GO2$log2FoldChange
names(kegg_gene_list_humanInt) <- humanInt_GO2$Y
kegg_gene_list_humanInt<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list_humanInt)
kegg_gene_list_humanInt = sort(kegg_gene_list_humanInt, decreasing = TRUE)

kegg_organism = "hsa"
kk2_humanInt <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list_humanInt,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               nPerm        = 10000,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",
               seed = T)


dotplot(kk2_humanInt, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
```

#### Compare together KEGG Pathways
```{r}
mlist <- list(kk2_maleHuman, kk2_femHuman, kk2_humanInt)
names(mlist) <- c("oldMales","oldFemales","Interaction")
mresults <- merge_result(mlist)
dotplot(mresults, showCategory=5, split=".sign", includeAll=T) +
  facet_grid(.~.sign) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1,)) +
  xlab("")

## let's try an upset and see what I get
maleHumanInAge_KEGG <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleHuman)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("KEGG.pathway", "MaleNES"))
femaleHumanInAge_KEGG <- as.data.frame(kk2_femHuman)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("KEGG.pathway", "FemaleNES"))
interactionHuman_KEGG <- as.data.frame(kk2_humanInt)[,c(3,6)] %>% 'rownames<-'(NULL) %>% set_names(c("KEGG.pathway", "InteractionNES"))

listInputs_1 <- list("Male_inAge" = maleHumanInAge_KEGG$KEGG.pathway,
                     "Female_inAge" = femaleHumanInAge_KEGG$KEGG.pathway,
                     "Interaction" =
                       interactionHuman_KEGG$KEGG.pathway)
upset(fromList(listInputs_1), sets = c("Interaction","Male_inAge","Female_inAge"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", mainbar.y.label = "Shared KEGG Pathways", sets.x.label = "Total KEGG Pathways")
```

#---------------------------
# Compative analysis
## Orthogroup comparison
```{r}
# load in the orthogroups information attained from OrthoFinder
orthogroups = read.delim("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/proteomes/primary_transcripts_secondRunDec27/OrthoFinder/Results_Dec27/Orthogroups/Orthogroups.tsv", header=T, stringsAsFactors = F)[,1:7] %>% #subsetted the data to exclude last column X maculatus
  pivot_longer(cols = -Orthogroup, names_to = "organism", values_to = "protein") %>%
  separate_longer_delim(cols = protein, delim = ", ") %>%
  filter(protein!="") %>%
  mutate(protein=str_remove(protein, "[.][1-3]"))
str(orthogroups)
# Make dataframe of DEGs, log fold changes, orthogroups
malefly_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(malefly_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.malefly=median(log2FoldChange))
femfly_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(femfly_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.femfly=median(log2FoldChange))
malehuman_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_human) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.malehuman=median(log2FoldChange))
femhuman_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_human) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.femhuman=median(log2FoldChange))
malemouse_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(malemouse_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_mouse) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.malemouse=median(log2FoldChange))
femmouse_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(femmouse_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_mouse) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.femmouse=median(log2FoldChange))
malerat_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(malerat_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_rat) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.malerat=median(log2FoldChange))
femrat_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(femrat_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>% 
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_rat) %>% 
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% 
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>% 
  summarise(medianl2fc.femrat=median(log2FoldChange))
malekillifish_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(malekillifish_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_killifish) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.malekillifish=median(log2FoldChange))
femkillifish_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(femkillifish_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_killifish) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.femkillifish=median(log2FoldChange))
malemoth_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(malemoth_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.malemoth=median(log2FoldChange))
femmoth_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(femmoth_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.femmoth=median(log2FoldChange))

# combine all dataframes above and search for comon orthogroups
full_join(malefly_resSig2, femfly_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(malehuman_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(femhuman_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(malemouse_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(femmouse_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(malerat_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(femrat_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(malekillifish_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(femkillifish_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(malemoth_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(femmoth_resSig2)-> allSigOrthogroups

view(allSigOrthogroups)
view(orthogroups)
dim(allSigOrthogroups) # 13,266 orthogroups 
# view(femFish_resSig_lfcShrink)
# write.xlsx(allSigOrthogroups, file = "/Users/school/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/allSigOrthogroups.xlsx")
```
###### table with OG and genes
```{r}
OrthogroupAdmissionSigGenes_H.sapiens_fem <- rownames_to_column(femhuman_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_human) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  dplyr::select(Orthogroup, gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  mutate(species = "H. sapiens", Sex = "female") %>%
  arrange(Orthogroup, desc(log2FoldChange)) %>% # stop here to get the names of the genes
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(
    upregulated = sum(log2FoldChange > 0),
    downregulated = sum(log2FoldChange < 0)
  ) %>% mutate(species = "H. sapiens", Sex = "female")

OrthogroupAdmissionSigGenes_H.sapiens_male <- rownames_to_column(malehuman_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_human) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  dplyr::select(Orthogroup, gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  mutate(species = "H. sapiens", Sex = "male") %>%
  arrange(Orthogroup, desc(log2FoldChange)) %>% # stop here to get the names of the genes
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(
    upregulated = sum(log2FoldChange > 0),
    downregulated = sum(log2FoldChange < 0)
  ) %>% mutate(species = "H. sapiens", Sex = "male")

# names(mlist2.5)<-c("f_H.sapiens", "m_H.sapiens", "f_M.musculus", "m_M.musculus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_N.furzeri", "m_N.furzeri", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_D.melanogaster", "m_D.melanogaster")

# 
# write.xlsx(as.data.frame(mresults2.5), file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/OrthogroupAdmissions(SigGenesOnly)11_17_25.xlsx", sheetName = "All", row.names = F)
```


#### same as above but for Interactions
```{r}
flyInt_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(flyInt_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.flyInt=median(log2FoldChange))
humanInt_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(humanInt_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_human) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.humanInt=median(log2FoldChange))
mouseInt_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(mouseInt_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_mouse) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.mouseInt=median(log2FoldChange))
ratInt_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(ratInt_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_rat) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.ratInt=median(log2FoldChange))
killifishInt_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(killifishInt_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_killifish) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.killifishInt=median(log2FoldChange))
mothInt_resSig2 <- rownames_to_column(mothInt_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(medianl2fc.mothInt=median(log2FoldChange))

full_join(flyInt_resSig2, humanInt_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(mouseInt_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(ratInt_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(killifishInt_resSig2) %>%
  full_join(mothInt_resSig2) -> allSigOrthogroups_Int 

View(allSigOrthogroups_Int)
dim(allSigOrthogroups_Int) # 2,073 orthogroups
```


## UpSetPlot
```{r}
#make upsetplot for this data 
## first make lists of all orthogroups in each species/sex
# maleFlyOrthogroupsList <- list(malefly_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# femFlyOrthogroupList <- list(femfly_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# maleHumanOrthogroupList <- list(malehuman_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# femHumanOrthogroupList <- list(femhuman_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# maleMouseOrthogroupList <- list(malemouse_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# femMouseOrthogroupList <- list(femmouse_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# maleRatOrthogroupList <- list(malerat_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# femRatOrthogroupList <- list(femrat_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# maleKillifishOrthogroupList <- list(malekillifish_resSig2$Orthogroup)
# femKillifeishOrthogroupList <- list(femkillifish_resSig2$Orthogroup)
## I did not actully need to make the lists above, in fact when I used the lists to make a list of inputs it did not play well with upsetplot. I was able to take this same design and make them individully as inputs and then run upset plot. As seen below:

listInputs <- list("m_H.sapiens" = malehuman_resSig2$Orthogroup, 
                   "f_H.sapiens" = femhuman_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "m_M.musculus" = malemouse_resSig2$Orthogroup, 
                   "f_M.musculus" = femmouse_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "m_R.norvegicus" = malerat_resSig2$Orthogroup, 
                   "f_R.norvegicus" = femrat_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "m_N.furzeri" = malekillifish_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "f_N.furzeri" = femkillifish_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "m_P.interpunctella" = malemoth_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "f_P.interpunctella" = femmoth_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                   "m_D.melanogaster" = malefly_resSig2$Orthogroup, 
                   "f_D.melanogaster" = femfly_resSig2$Orthogroup
                   )
#The upset plot only displayed 5 of the samples so i had to specify I wanted all samples displayed by using the "sets=" command below
## I can order by "order.by=" "freq" which puts the sample/samples with the most genes first, but as I am interested in common orthologs I set my order by to "degree" which put the most common orthologs first.
## also, when copying the graph it I found it best to save to pdf custom landscape 6x12
UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputs), sets=c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus",  "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), order.by = "freq", keep.order = T, cutoff = 10, nintersects = 20, sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")

## Now I will make 2 more upset plots with only upregulated and only downreguleted ortholog genes
## lets make the upregulated plots first
### fisr I will filter out the upregulated orthogroups by making a new object of significant orthogroups, making the column "Orthogroups" into rownames, and then filtering those rows that have a medianl2fc > 0.
#### I then make my listInput by making a list of the rownames I of the rows I just filtered
maleflyUp <- malefly_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malefly > 0)
femflyUp <- femfly_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femfly > 0)
malehumanUp <- malehuman_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malehuman > 0)
femhumanUp <- femhuman_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femhuman > 0)
malemouseUp <- malemouse_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malemouse > 0)
femmouseUp <- femmouse_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femmouse > 0)
maleratUp <- malerat_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malerat > 0)
femratUp <- femrat_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femrat > 0)
malekillifishUp <- malekillifish_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malekillifish > 0)
femkillifishUp <- femkillifish_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femkillifish > 0)
malemothUp <- malemoth_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malemoth > 0)
femmothUp <- femmoth_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femmoth > 0)

listInputsUP <- list("m_H.sapiens" = rownames(malehumanUp),
                     "f_H.sapiens" = rownames(femhumanUp),
                     "m_M.musculus" = rownames(malemouseUp),
                     "f_M.musculus" = rownames(femmouseUp),
                     "m_R.norvegicus" = rownames(maleratUp),
                     "f_R.norvegicus" = rownames(femratUp),
                     "m_N.furzeri" = rownames(malekillifishUp),
                     "f_N.furzeri" = rownames(femkillifishUp),
                     "m_P.interpunctella" = rownames(malemothUp),
                     "f_P.interpunctella" = rownames(femmothUp),
                     "m_D.melanogaster" = rownames(maleflyUp),
                     "f_D.melanogaster" = rownames(femflyUp)
                     )

UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputsUP), sets=c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus",  "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), order.by = c("freq"), keep.order = T, cutoff = 10, nintersects = 20, sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")


### Cool Cool, now let's get the downregulated orthogroups
maleflyDown <- malefly_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malefly < 0)
femflyDown <- femfly_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femfly < 0)
malehumanDown <- malehuman_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malehuman < 0)
femhumanDown <- femhuman_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femhuman < 0)
malemouseDown <- malemouse_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malemouse < 0)
femmouseDown <- femmouse_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femmouse < 0)
maleratDown <- malerat_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malerat < 0)
femratDown <- femrat_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femrat < 0)
malekillifishDown <- malekillifish_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malekillifish < 0)
femkillifishDown <- femkillifish_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femkillifish < 0)
malemothDown <- malemoth_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.malemoth < 0)
femmothDown <- femmoth_resSig2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Orthogroup") %>%
  filter(medianl2fc.femmoth < 0)

listInputsDown <- list("m_H.sapiens" = rownames(malehumanDown),
                       "f_H.sapiens" = rownames(femhumanDown),
                       "m_M.musculus" = rownames(malemouseDown),
                       "f_M.musculus" = rownames(femmouseDown),
                       "m_R.norvegicus" = rownames(maleratDown),
                       "f_R.norvegicus" = rownames(femratDown),
                       "m_N.furzeri" = rownames(malekillifishDown),
                       "f_N.furzeri" = rownames(femkillifishDown),
                       "m_P.interpunctella" = rownames(malemothDown),
                       "f_P.interpunctella" = rownames(femmothDown),
                       "m_D.melanogaster" = rownames(maleflyDown),
                       "f_D.melanogaster" = rownames(femflyDown)
                       )

UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputsDown), sets=c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus",  "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), order.by = c("freq"), keep.order = T, cutoff = 10, nintersects = 20, sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")
```

#### Interactions
```{r}
listInputsInt <- list("H.sapiens_Int" = humanInt_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                      "M.musculus_Int" = mouseInt_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                      "R.norvegicus_Int" = ratInt_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                      "N.furzeri_Int" = killifishInt_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                      "P.interpunctella_Int" = mothInt_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                      "D.melanogaster_Int" = flyInt_resSig2$Orthogroup
                      )
test <- UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputsInt), sets=c("H.sapiens_Int", "M.musculus_Int", "R.norvegicus_Int", "N.furzeri_Int", "P.interpunctella_Int", "D.melanogaster_Int"), order.by = "degree", keep.order = T, cutoff = 10, nintersects = 200, sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")
test$New_data
x4 <- unlist(listInputsInt, use.names = FALSE)
x4 <- x4[ !duplicated(x4) ]
x4[ rowSums(test$New_data) == 3 ] 
```

```{r}
# Google said I could try using the traditiinal upSet
## original plot below
UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputs), sets=c("m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens"), order.by = "degree", keep.order = T, cutoff = 10, nintersects = 20, sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")


## Both sexes
UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputs), 
              intersections = list(c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus","m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri","m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), #ALL
                                   #c("f_H.sapiens","f_M.musculus","f_R.norvegicus","f_N.furzeri","f_P.interpunctella","f_D.melanogaster"), #Females
                                   #c("m_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus","m_N.furzeri","m_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster"), #Males
                                   c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #mammals
                                   c("m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #rodents
                                   c("m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), #insects
                                   c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens"), #humans
                                   c("m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus"), #mouse
                                   c("m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #rat
                                   c("m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri"), #killifish
                                   c("m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella"), #moth
                                   c("m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster") #fly
                                   ),
              sets=c("m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")

######
## Above gave me what I wanted but it didn't show sex specific because there were none that only showed in males or only in females. Below I will subset the data by sex and graph to sisplay this

#First make a list of sex-specific orthogroup apperance
listInputsFemales2 <- list("f_H.sapiens" = femhuman_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                           "f_M.musculus" = femmouse_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                           "f_R.norvegicus" = femrat_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                           "f_N.furzeri" = femkillifish_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                           "f_P.interpunctella" = femmoth_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                           "f_D.melanogaster" = femfly_resSig2$Orthogroup)
 

listInputsMales2 <- list("m_H.sapiens" = malehuman_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                         "m_M.musculus" = malemouse_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                         "m_R.norvegicus" = malerat_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                         "m_N.furzeri" = malekillifish_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                         "m_P.interpunctella" = malemoth_resSig2$Orthogroup,
                         "m_D.melanogaster" = malefly_resSig2$Orthogroup)

## Females Only
UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputsFemales2), sets=c("f_D.melanogaster", "f_P.interpunctella", "f_N.furzeri", "f_R.norvegicus", "f_M.musculus", "f_H.sapiens"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG",
              intersections = list(
                c("f_H.sapiens","f_M.musculus","f_R.norvegicus","f_N.furzeri","f_P.interpunctella","f_D.melanogaster"), #Females
                                   c("f_H.sapiens","f_M.musculus","f_R.norvegicus"), #mammals
                
c("f_M.musculus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #rodents
                
c("f_P.interpunctella", "f_D.melanogaster"))) #insects



## Males Only
UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputsMales2), 
              intersections = list(
                c("m_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus","m_N.furzeri","m_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster"), #Males
                                   c("m_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus"), #mammals
                
c("m_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus"), #rodents
                                   c("m_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster")), #insects
              sets=c("m_D.melanogaster", "m_P.interpunctella", "m_N.furzeri", "m_R.norvegicus", "m_M.musculus", "m_H.sapiens"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")
```
#####get names OGs
```{r}
## first we will make our upset plot an object
getOrthogroups <- UpSetR::upset(UpSetR::fromList(listInputs), 
              intersections = list(c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus","m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri","m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), #ALL
                                   #c("f_H.sapiens","f_M.musculus","f_R.norvegicus","f_N.furzeri","f_P.interpunctella","f_D.melanogaster"), #Females
                                   #c("m_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus","m_N.furzeri","m_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster"), #Males
                                   c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens","m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #mammals
                                   c("m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus","m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #rodents
                                   c("m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella","m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster"), #insects
                                   c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens"), #humans
                                   c("m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus"), #mouse
                                   c("m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus"), #rat
                                   c("m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri"), #killifish
                                   c("m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella"), #moth
                                   c("m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster") #fly
                                   ),
              sets=c("m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens"), keep.order = T, order.by = "degree", sets.x.label = " # of Orthogroups containing \nan associated DEG", mainbar.y.label = "# of Shared Orthogroups \ncontaining an associated DEG")

## within the object we just make we can get a dataframe that has binanry inclusions (intersection) we can use the rowSums of each row (rows correspond to the Orthogroups) to get the specific intersections we are looking for.... but we also need to take into consideration which column we want counted 
getOrthogroups$New_data 

## now we take the list of lists of orthogroups we made that contained all species and unlisted it
x2 <- unlist(listInputs, use.names = FALSE)
x2 <- x2[ !duplicated(x2) ] ## then got rid of duplicates 13,266 0rthogroups

## Then we subset the orthogrouplist according to the intersection we wanted (called by the amount of rowSums and which sex/species we wanted included)
## In all of them
ubiqutousAll <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 12 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_D.melanogaster == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_D.melanogaster == 1 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_P.interpunctella == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_P.interpunctella == 1 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_N.furzeri == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_N.furzeri == 1 &
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_R.norvegicus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_R.norvegicus == 1 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_M.musculus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_M.musculus == 1 &
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_H.sapiens == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_H.sapiens == 1]
## below is an easy code to get the all inclusive ones
x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 12 ] 
Reduce(intersect, listInputs)



## Now let's get the ubiquitous intersections for each sex (sex-biased)
listInputsFemales2 # came from the code chunk above
## below is an easy code to get only the all inclusive ones. Since we are only interested in the ubiquitous ones at this point I will not make the other upset plots into objects and look at the specifics as I did above.
ubiqutousFemalesOnly <- Reduce(intersect, listInputsFemales2)

# for males
listInputsMales2 # came from the code chunk above
ubiqutousMalesOnly <- Reduce(intersect, listInputsMales2)

```



Same as above but for all the other intersections with all data. Getting the names of Orthogroups
```{r}
# Mammal specific
mammalSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 6 &
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_R.norvegicus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_R.norvegicus == 1 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_M.musculus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_M.musculus == 1 &
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_H.sapiens == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_H.sapiens == 1]


# rodent specific
rodentSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 4 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_R.norvegicus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_R.norvegicus == 1 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_M.musculus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_M.musculus == 1]


# Insect specific
insectSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 4 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_D.melanogaster == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_D.melanogaster == 1 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_P.interpunctella == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_P.interpunctella == 1]


# human specific
humanSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 2 &
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_H.sapiens == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_H.sapiens == 1]



# mouse specific
mouseSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 2 &  
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_M.musculus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_M.musculus == 1]



# Rat specific
ratSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 2 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_R.norvegicus == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_R.norvegicus == 1]



# Killifish specific
killifishSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 2 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_N.furzeri == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_N.furzeri == 1]




# moth specific
mothSpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 2 &  
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_P.interpunctella == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_P.interpunctella == 1]



# fly specific
flySpecific <- x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 2 & 
                      getOrthogroups$New_data$m_D.melanogaster == 1 & getOrthogroups$New_data$f_D.melanogaster == 1 ]



# write.xlsx(as.data.frame(flySpecific), file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/upSetPlotResults(ALL)12_01_25.xlsx", sheetName = "flySpecific", row.names = F, append = T)

## below is another way to get a list of the intersection size terms but only if you want those terms that have full contribution from all species. I don't think this is as useful as the one baobe but Still, really clean code!
## sanity check
x2[ rowSums(getOrthogroups$New_data) == 12 ] 
Reduce(intersect, listInputs)

```

##### function for below
```{r}
# I want a list of all genes that fall into the orthogroup with the ones that are DEGs to have an * by thier names. I want this in one cell of a data frame with the column heade being the sex:species
#install.packages("openxlsx")
#library(openxlsx)

# below is a function that will give me the list I am looking for
# 2. Process data in R to combine and bold conditionally
process_data <- function(gene, Reg) {
  combined_string <- ""
  for (i in seq_along(gene)) {
    genes <- gene[i]
    # Check the condition from the other column
    if (Reg[i] != "not") {
      # A more robust approach might be to use a simpler marker or accept limitations.
      bold_item <- paste0(genes, "* ") # Use asterisks as a visual indicator
      combined_string <- paste0(combined_string, bold_item, ", ") # Add a newline character
    } else {
      combined_string <- paste0(combined_string, genes, ", ")
    }
  }
  # Remove trailing comma at the end
  return(gsub(", $", "", combined_string)) 
}
## Now I will run the function
#final_cell_value <- process_data(O_maleH.sapiens$gene, O_maleH.sapiens$Reg)

## this is not for above. this was for the part below
## So what if I now make a data frame for these lists on my own
#dfTest <- data.frame(Orthogroup = "OG0000071") # creates a data frame
#dfTest$male_H.sapiens <-  final_cell_value # Adds a column with the string I made above
```


##### Loop for ataining genes within the OGs per species/sex which also indicates which genes were DEGs in age
```{r}
## Will make a list of all the data frames and name them by orthogroup
testList = list() # I restart my list every time
string_list <- flySpecific #use object created in "get names OGs" section above
for (i in seq_along(string_list)) {
  current_string <- string_list[i]
########
########
O_maleFly <- rownames_to_column(malefly_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == string_list[i]) %>% 
  mutate(Sex="male")
O_maleD.melanogaster <- lfcShrink(fly_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == string_list[i]) %>% 
  # distinct(gene, .keep_all = T) %>% # keeps only unique genes
  tidyr::unite(col = "gene_protein", gene, protein, sep=":", remove = F) %>% # makes new column with gene:proteinID
  mutate(Sex="male") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% O_maleFly$gene, 
                                             "DEG_M","not"))
final_cell_value <- process_data(O_maleD.melanogaster$gene_protein, O_maleD.melanogaster$Reg)
dfGeneAddmitance <- data.frame(Orthogroup = string_list[i]) # creates the df ***
dfGeneAddmitance$male_D.melanogaster <-  final_cell_value


O_femFly <- rownames_to_column(femfly_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == string_list[i]) %>% 
  mutate(Sex="female")
O_femD.melanogaster <- lfcShrink(fly_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>%

  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == string_list[i]) %>% 
  # distinct(gene, .keep_all = T) %>% # keeps only unique genes
  tidyr::unite(col = "gene_protein", gene, protein, sep=":", remove = F) %>% # makes new column with gene:proteinID
  mutate(Sex="female") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% O_femFly$gene, 
                                             "DEG_F","not"))
final_cell_value <- process_data(O_femD.melanogaster$gene_protein, O_femD.melanogaster$Reg)
dfGeneAddmitance$female_D.melanogaster <-  final_cell_value

                                             "DEG_F","not"))
final_cell_value <- process_data(O_femH.sapiens$gene_protein, O_femH.sapiens$Reg)
dfGeneAddmitance$female_H.sapiens <-  final_cell_value
######
######
## this adds the complete dataframe to my named list
testList[[string_list[i]]] = dfGeneAddmitance
}

testdf <- testList %>% do.call(rbind,.) # This makes the list of data frames into a single dataframe, not multiple sheets]
openxlsx::write.xlsx(testdf, file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/_V7_noXmac/UpsetOrthogroupsWithGenes_tables/flySpecific.xlsx", overwrite = T) #this saves it

# openXL(wb)
# saveWorkbook(wb, file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/TestingStuff.xlsx", overwrite = T)

# do.call(rbind) <- look into this for binding the dataframes  

```






##Graphing paralog/isoform DEGs in Orthogroups and saving dataframes of inclusions and DEGs
####Both Sexes
######OG0000027
```{r}
## This orthogroup number is OG0000027 and includes proteins of Actin

orthogroups %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% filter(organism=="D_melanogaster") # 8 proteins in this orthogroup
actin_maleFly <- rownames_to_column(malefly_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="male")
actin_maleD.melanogaster <- lfcShrink(fly_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>% 
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>% 
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  tidyr::unite(col = "gene_protein", gene, protein, sep=":", remove = F) %>% # makes new column with gene:proteinID
  mutate(Sex="male") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% actin_maleFly$gene, 
                                             "DEG_M","not"))
actin_femFly <- rownames_to_column(femfly_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="female")
actin_femD.melanogaster <- lfcShrink(fly_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_fly) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  tidyr::unite(col = "gene_protein", gene, protein, sep=":", remove = F) %>% # makes new column with gene:proteinID
  mutate(Sex="female") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% actin_femFly$gene, 
                                             "DEG_F","not"))

orthogroups %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% filter(organism=="P_interpunctella") # 7 proteins in this orthogroup
actin_maleMoth <- rownames_to_column(malemoth_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="male")

  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  tidyr::unite(col = "gene_protein", gene, protein, sep=":", remove = F) %>% # makes new column with gene:proteinID
  mutate(Sex="male") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% actin_maleMoth$gene, 
                                             "DEG_M","not"))
actin_femMoth <- rownames_to_column(femmoth_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="female")
actin_femP.interpunctella <- lfcShrink(moth_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>%

  mutate(Sex="female") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene

orthogroups %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000027") %>% filter(organism=="N_furzeri") # 10 proteins in this orthogroup
```
