```{r}
## Similar issue as humans where one protein's gene is missing (12 -> 11) from the detectable results. After viewing the DESeq data this gene is not present, suggesting it was not detected in this tissue. The protein was XP_054591666 and the gene was LOC129156071.

orthogroups %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000071") %>% filter(organism=="P_interpunctella") # 8 proteins in this orthogroup
hsp70_maleMoth <- rownames_to_column(malemoth_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000071") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="male")
hsp70_maleP.interpunctella <- lfcShrink(moth_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldmale", "youngmale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000071") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="male") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% hsp70_maleMoth$gene, 
                                             "DEG_M","not"))
hsp70_femMoth <- rownames_to_column(femmoth_resSig_lfcShrink,"gene") %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000071") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="female")
hsp70_femP.interpunctella <- lfcShrink(moth_dds2, contrast = c("group", "oldfemale", "youngfemale"), type = "ashr", alpha = 0.05, lfcThreshold = 0) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(gene = str_remove(gene, "\\..*")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, log2FoldChange) %>%
  inner_join(gene2tx2protein_moth) %>%
  left_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup == "OG0000071") %>% 
  mutate(Sex="female") %>% mutate(Reg=ifelse(gene
                                             %in% hsp70_femMoth$gene, 
                                             "DEG_F","not"))
# combine all dataframes
full_join(hsp70_maleD.melanogaster, hsp70_femD.melanogaster) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleH.sapiens) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femH.sapiens) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleM.musculus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femM.musculus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleR.norvegicus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femR.norvegicus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleN.furzeri) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femN.furzeri) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleP.interpunctella) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femP.interpunctella) -> allhsp70expression
#View(allhsp70expression)

allhsp70expression$organism <- factor(allhsp70expression$organism, levels = rev(c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")))


# orignial graph with only sig genes
allhsp70expression_SigOnly <- allhsp70expression[!grepl("not", allhsp70expression$Reg),]
allhsp70expression_SigOnly$organism <- factor(allhsp70expression_SigOnly$organism, levels=rev(c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")))
ggplot(allhsp70expression_SigOnly, aes(x=organism, y=log2FoldChange, fill=Sex)) +
  geom_boxplot()  +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("grey", "grey20","grey","grey20","grey", "grey20","grey", "grey20","grey", "grey20","grey", "grey20")) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x="", y="log2 fold change", title = "Paralog/Isoform DEGs within hsp70 orthogroup") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=10, color="black"),
        axis.title = element_text(size=14),
        title = element_text(size=12, face="bold")) +
  guides(x = guide_axis(angle = 45)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Sex), position=position_jitterdodge(jitter.height = 0, jitter.width = 0.01)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype=2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=1, linetype=2, color="grey90") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=-1, linetype=2, color="grey90")

# original graph with more points for non-sig genes
M_hsp70 <- allhsp70expression[allhsp70expression$Sex=="male",] #subset by sex for points
F_hsp70 <- allhsp70expression[allhsp70expression$Sex=="female",]
allhsp70expression$organism <- factor(allhsp70expression$organism, levels=rev(c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")))
ggplot(allhsp70expression, aes(x=organism, y=log2FoldChange, fill=Sex)) +
  geom_boxplot()  +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("grey", "grey20","grey","grey20","grey", "grey20","grey", "grey20","grey", "grey20","grey", "grey20")) +
  theme_classic() +
  guides(fill="none") +
  labs(x="", y="log2 fold change", title = "Paralog/Isoform genes associated with the hsp70 orthogroup") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=10, color="black"),
        axis.title = element_text(size=14),
        title = element_text(size=12, face="bold")) +
  guides(x = guide_axis(angle = 45)) +
  geom_point(data=M_hsp70, aes(color=Reg), position=position_nudge(x=0.19)) +
  geom_point(data=F_hsp70, aes(color=Reg), position=position_nudge(x=-0.19)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("DEG_F"="#F8766D", "DEG_M"="#00BFC4", "not"="cornsilk4"), labels = c("DEG_F"="female DEG", "DEG_M"="male DEG", "not"="not sig")) +
  labs(color = "Genes in orthogroup") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype=2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=1, linetype=2, color="grey90") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=-1, linetype=2, color="grey90")

## new graph
ggplot(allhsp70expression, aes(x=organism, y=log2FoldChange, fill=Sex)) +
  geom_point(data=M_hsp70, aes(color=Reg), position=position_nudge(x=0.19), size = 4) +
  geom_point(data=F_hsp70, aes(color=Reg), position=position_nudge(x=-0.19), size = 4) +
  theme_classic() +
  guides(fill="none") +
  labs(x="", y="log2 fold change", title = "Paralog/Isoform genes associated with the hsp70 orthogroup") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=10, color="black"),
        axis.title = element_text(size=14),
        title = element_text(size=12, face="bold")) +
  guides(x = guide_axis(angle = 45)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("DEG_F"="#F8766D", "DEG_M"="#00BFC4", "not"="grey"), labels = c("DEG_F"="female DEG", "DEG_M"="male DEG", "not"="not sig")) +
  labs(color = "Genes in orthogroup")  +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype=2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=1, linetype=2, color="grey90") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=-1, linetype=2, color="grey90")
```

#### save data frames
```{r}
## I think I already did this above lol, oh well.
## Never mind, this was needed to re-order the species and sex to match the rest of the manuscript
#SERCA_femH.sapiens
#SERCA_maleH.sapiens
#SERCA_femM.musculus
#SERCA_maleM.musculus
# SERCA_femR.norvegicus
#SERCA_maleR.norvegicus
# SERCA_femN.furzeri
# SERCA_maleN.furzeri
# SERCA_femP.interpunctella
# SERCA_maleP.interpunctella
# SERCA_femD.melanogaster
# SERCA_maleD.melanogaster
full_join(SERCA_femH.sapiens, SERCA_maleH.sapiens) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_femM.musculus) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_maleM.musculus) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_femR.norvegicus) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_maleR.norvegicus) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_femN.furzeri) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_maleN.furzeri) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_femP.interpunctella) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_maleP.interpunctella) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_femD.melanogaster) %>%
  full_join(SERCA_maleD.melanogaster) -> SERCAgenesListInOG

# write.xlsx(SERCAgenesListInOG, file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/SERCAgenesListInOG_12_10_25.xlsx", sheetName = "SERCA_OG", row.names = F)

# hsp70_femH.sapiens
# hsp70_maleH.sapiens
# hsp70_femM.musculus
# hsp70_maleM.musculus
# hsp70_femR.norvegicus
# hsp70_maleR.norvegicus
# hsp70_femN.furzeri
# hsp70_maleN.furzeri
# hsp70_femP.interpunctella
# hsp70_maleP.interpunctella
# hsp70_femD.melanogaster
# hsp70_maleD.melanogaster

full_join(hsp70_femH.sapiens, hsp70_maleH.sapiens) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femM.musculus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleM.musculus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femR.norvegicus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleR.norvegicus) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femN.furzeri) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleN.furzeri) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femP.interpunctella) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleP.interpunctella) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_femD.melanogaster) %>%
  full_join(hsp70_maleD.melanogaster) -> HSP70genesListInOG

#write.xlsx(HSP70genesListInOG, file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/HSP70genesListInOG_12_10_25.xlsx", sheetName = "HSP70_OG", row.names = F)
```

###-------
#### Chi^2 on DEGs
```{r}
## here we want to statistically check if there is and difference in up- vs downregulated gene within a species per contrast (sex)

## we want to do a Chi-Squared Goodness of Fit Test: to determine if the observed counts differ from a 50/50 distribution.... below is an example and how to set up
# Example: Test if observed counts match a uniform distribution (0.2 for each)
observed_counts <- c(36, 44, 38, 37, 45)
expected_probs <- c(0.20, 0.20, 0.20, 0.20, 0.20)

goodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(observed_counts, p = expected_probs)
print(goodness_of_fit_result)

# D. melanogaster
## males ** statistically sig p = 0.002037 (more upregulated genes)
mFly_observed_counts <- c(1146, 1003)
mFly_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
mFlyGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(mFly_observed_counts, p = mFly_expected_probs)
print(mFlyGoodness_of_fit_result)
## females ** statistically sig p < 2.2e-16 (more upregulated genes)
fFly_observed_counts <- c(1639, 1199)
fFly_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
fFlyGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(fFly_observed_counts, p = fFly_expected_probs)
print(fFlyGoodness_of_fit_result)

# P. interpunctella
## males ** statistically sig p = 0.02821 (more downregulated genes)
mMoth_observed_counts <- c(2951, 3122)
mMoth_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
mMothGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(mMoth_observed_counts, p = mMoth_expected_probs)
print(mMothGoodness_of_fit_result)
## females ** not significantly diff
fMoth_observed_counts <- c(2526, 2458)
fMoth_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
fMothGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(fMoth_observed_counts, p = fMoth_expected_probs)
print(fMothGoodness_of_fit_result)

# N. furzeri
## males ** statistically sig p = 0.01666 (more downregulated genes)
mKillifish_observed_counts <- c(1862, 2011)
mKillifish_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
mKillifishGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(mKillifish_observed_counts, p = mKillifish_expected_probs)
print(mKillifishGoodness_of_fit_result)
## females ** statistically sig p = 0.01296 (more upregulated genes)
fKillifish_observed_counts <- c(1246, 1125)
fKillifish_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
fKillifishGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(fKillifish_observed_counts, p = fKillifish_expected_probs)
print(fKillifishGoodness_of_fit_result)

# R. norvegicus
## males ** statistically sig p = 3.395e-12 (more downregulated genes)
mRat_observed_counts <- c(3001, 3565)
mRat_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
mRatGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(mRat_observed_counts, p = mRat_expected_probs)
print(mRatGoodness_of_fit_result)
## females ** statistically sig p = 3.26e-08 (more downregulated genes)
fRat_observed_counts <- c(2651, 3069)
fRat_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
fRatGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(fRat_observed_counts, p = fRat_expected_probs)
print(fRatGoodness_of_fit_result)

# M. Musculus
## males ** not sig diff
mMouse_observed_counts <- c(2085, 2036)
mMouse_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
mMouseGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(mMouse_observed_counts, p = mMouse_expected_probs)
print(mMouseGoodness_of_fit_result)
## females ** statistically sig p = 2.2e-16 (more upregulated genes)
fMouse_observed_counts <- c(1954, 1456)
fMouse_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
fMouseGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(fMouse_observed_counts, p = fMouse_expected_probs)
print(fMouseGoodness_of_fit_result)

# H. sapiens
## males ** statistically sig p = 0.0008761 (more downregulated genes)
mHuman_observed_counts <- c(1502, 1690)
mHuman_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
mHumanGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(mHuman_observed_counts, p = mHuman_expected_probs)
print(mHumanGoodness_of_fit_result)
## females ** statistically sig p = 0.001143 (more downregulated genes)
fHuman_observed_counts <- c(2077, 2292)
fHuman_expected_probs <- c(0.50, 0.50)
fHumanGoodness_of_fit_result <- chisq.test(fHuman_observed_counts, p = fHuman_expected_probs)
print(fHumanGoodness_of_fit_result)
```


#___________________
## Combine and graph GO&KEGG
#### GO Age between Sexes
```{r}
# GO terms
## male GO
mlist1 <- list(gse_maleHuman, gse_maleMouse, gse_maleRat, gse_maleKillifish, gse_malemoth, gse_maleFly)
names(mlist1)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
mresults1<-merge_result(mlist1)
dotplot(mresults1, showCategory=2, split=".sign", includeAll=T) + facet_grid(.sign~.) + # when (.~.sign) = side-by-side ; When (.sign~.) = on-top
  coord_flip() + #rotates entire plot 90 degrees
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 

## making a differnet graph to be comparable to the one above but with activated and suppressed on the same graph wthout a facet wrap
## start by reproducing the above graph
mlist1.2 <- list(gse_maleHuman, gse_maleMouse, gse_maleRat, gse_maleKillifish, gse_malemoth, gse_maleFly)
# now name all the dataframes in the list with appropriate sex and species
names(mlist1.2)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
# now merge all the results/datagframes in the list
mresults1.2<-merge_result(mlist1.2)
# now make the results a dataframe, calculate a geneRatio, and define the sign
mresults1.2df <- as.data.frame(mresults1.2) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated")) 
# now group the data frame by species, index each row within each group, and arrage by index
mresults1.2dfb <- mresults1.2df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
# Now lets subset the dataframe to only inclued the first 8 rows of each species which corresponds to the 4 most significant (by p.adjust) GO terms for that species
###mresults1.2dfb_subSet <- mresults1.2dfb %>% slice(1:2) # this just gives me the most significant based on p.adj I want both activated and suppressed for lowest p.adj
## I will try this
# First define my target strings 
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults1.2dfb_subSet <- mresults1.2dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
# Now lets add any rows from other species that have the same Description in the sliced dataframe
mresults1.2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults1.2dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults1.2dfb_subSet$Description) 

# let's order the species by phylogeny (insects, fish, mammals)
mresults1.2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults1.2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("D_melanogaster", "P_interpunctella", "N_furzeri", "R_norvegicus", "M_musculus", "H_sapiens")))
# make sign s factor
mresults1.2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults1.2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults1.2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "males only") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(30))


## Female GO
# mlist2 <- list(gse_femFly, gse_femHuman, gse_femMouse, gse_femRat, gse_femKillifish)
# names(mlist2)<-c("Fly","Human","Mouse","Rat","Killifish")
# mresults2<-merge_result(mlist2)
# dotplot(mresults2, showCategory=2, split=".sign", includeAll=T) + facet_grid(.~.sign)
mlist2 <- list(gse_femHuman, gse_femMouse, gse_femRat, gse_femKillifish, gse_femmoth, gse_femFly)
names(mlist2)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
mresults2<-merge_result(mlist2)
mresults2df <- as.data.frame(mresults2) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults2dfb <- mresults2df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults2dfb_subSet <- mresults2dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults2dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults2dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("D_melanogaster", "P_interpunctella", "N_furzeri", "R_norvegicus", "M_musculus", "H_sapiens")))
mresults2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults2dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "females only") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(30))

## Combine the two graphs above
#mlist9 <- list(gse_femFly, gse_maleFly, gse_femHuman, gse_maleHuman, gse_femMouse, gse_maleMouse, gse_femRat, gse_maleRat, gse_femKillifish, gse_maleKillifish, gse_femmoth, gse_malemoth)
mlist3 <- list(gse_maleHuman, gse_femHuman, gse_maleMouse, gse_femMouse, gse_maleRat, gse_femRat, gse_maleKillifish, gse_femKillifish, gse_malemoth, gse_femmoth, gse_maleFly, gse_femFly)
names(mlist3)<-c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster")
mresults3<-merge_result(mlist3)
# ## I saved all the GO terms from all the species/sexes in one excel below
# write.xlsx(as.data.frame(mresults3), file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/GOterms(all)11_4_25.xlsx", sheetName = "GOall", row.names = F)
# ##
mresults3df <- as.data.frame(mresults3) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults3dfb <- mresults3df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults3dfb_subSet <- mresults3dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults3dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults3dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults3dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults3dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults3dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("f_D.melanogaster", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_N.furzeri", "m_N.furzeri", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_M.musculus", "m_M.musculus", "f_H.sapiens", "m_H.sapiens")))
mresults3dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults3dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults3dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="#DC3220", "suppressed"="#005AB5")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(40))
```

##### subsets "Mitocho"
```{r}
# below gives back only GO terms that have "mitoch" in them. but they are in dataframes (this may be prefered)
# let's now make one for all species/sex
gse_maleHumanMito <- gse_maleHuman[grep("mitoch", 
                                        gse_maleHuman@result$Description, 
                                        ignore.case = T),]
gse_femHumanMito <- gse_femHuman[grep("mitoch",
                                      gse_femHuman@result$Description,
                                      ignore.case = T),]
gse_maleMouseMito <- gse_maleMouse[grep("mitoch", 
                                        gse_maleMouse@result$Description, 
                                        ignore.case = T),]
gse_femMouseMito <- gse_femMouse[grep("mitoch",
                                      gse_femMouse@result$Description,
                                      ignore.case = T),]
gse_maleRatMito <- gse_maleRat[grep("mitoch", 
                                        gse_maleRat@result$Description, 
                                        ignore.case = T),]
gse_femRatMito <- gse_femRat[grep("mitoch",
                                      gse_femRat@result$Description,
                                      ignore.case = T),]
gse_maleKillifishMito <- gse_maleKillifish[grep("mitoch", 
                                        gse_maleKillifish@result$Description, 
                                        ignore.case = T),]
gse_femKillifishMito <- gse_femKillifish[grep("mitoch",
                                      gse_femKillifish@result$Description,
                                      ignore.case = T),]
gse_malemothMito <- gse_malemoth[grep("mitoch", 
                                        gse_malemoth@result$Description, 
                                        ignore.case = T),]
gse_femmothMito <- gse_femmoth[grep("mitoch",
                                      gse_femmoth@result$Description,
                                      ignore.case = T),]
gse_maleFlyMito <- gse_maleFly[grep("mitoch", 
                                        gse_maleFly@result$Description, 
                                        ignore.case = T),]
gse_femFlyMito <- gse_femFly[grep("mitoch",
                                      gse_femFly@result$Description,
                                      ignore.case = T),]
mlist_mito <- list(gse_maleHumanMito, gse_femHumanMito, gse_maleMouseMito, gse_femMouseMito, gse_maleRatMito, gse_femRatMito, gse_maleKillifishMito, gse_femKillifishMito, gse_malemothMito, gse_femmothMito, gse_maleFlyMito, gse_femFlyMito)
names(mlist_mito)<-c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster")
mresults_mito<-merge_result(mlist_mito)
mresults_mitodf <- as.data.frame(mresults_mito) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults_mitodfb <- mresults_mitodf %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults_mitodfb_subSet <- mresults_mitodfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults_mitodfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults_mitodfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults_mitodfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults_mitodfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults_mitodfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("f_D.melanogaster", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_N.furzeri", "m_N.furzeri", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_M.musculus", "m_M.musculus", "f_H.sapiens", "m_H.sapiens")))
mresults_mitodfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults_mitodfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

mresults_mitodfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(40))
```

#### GO Interactions (maleOld - maleYoung) - (femaleOld - femaleYoung)
```{r}
# mlist13 <- list(gse_flyInt, gse_humanInt, gse_mouseInt, gse_ratInt, gse_killifishInt, gse_mothInt)
# names(mlist13)<-c("Human","Mouse","Rat","Killifish","Moth","Fly")
# mresults13<-merge_result(mlist13)
# dotplot(mresults13, showCategory=2, split=".sign", includeAll=T) + 
#   facet_grid(.sign~.) +
#   coord_flip() + #rotates entire plot 90 degrees
#   theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5), 
#         axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 70, hjust = 1))
## AWESOME! so there is enough here to keep going and make a better graph! Yeah!!!
mlist4 <- list(gse_humanInt, gse_mouseInt, gse_ratInt, gse_killifishInt, gse_mothInt, gse_flyInt)
names(mlist4)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
mresults4<-merge_result(mlist4)
mresults4df <- as.data.frame(mresults4) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults4dfb <- mresults4df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults4dfb_subSet <- mresults4dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults4dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults4dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults4dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults4dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults4dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("D_melanogaster", "P_interpunctella", "N_furzeri", "R_norvegicus", "M_musculus", "H_sapiens")))
mresults4dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults4dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults4dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "Interactions only") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(30))
```


#### KEGG Age between Sexes
```{r}
# KEGG pathways
######################################################
######################################################
######################################################
# fist get rid of all the species specific indicators in the Description in the kk2 objects
kk2_femFly2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_femFly) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_femHuman2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_femHuman) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_femMouse2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_femMouse) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_femRat2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_femRat) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_femKillifish2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_femKillifish) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_femmoth2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_femmoth) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))


kk2_maleFly2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleFly) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_maleHuman2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleHuman) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_maleMouse2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleMouse) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_maleRat2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleRat) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_maleKillifish2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_maleKillifish) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_malemoth2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_malemoth) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))

## Males Only
mlist5 <- list(kk2_maleHuman2, kk2_maleMouse2, kk2_maleRat2, kk2_maleKillifish2, kk2_malemoth2, kk2_maleFly2)
names(mlist5)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
mresults5<-merge_result(mlist5)
mresults5df <- as.data.frame(mresults5) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults5dfb <- mresults5df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults5dfb_subSet <- mresults5dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults5dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults5dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("D_melanogaster", "P_interpunctella", "N_furzeri", "R_norvegicus", "M_musculus", "H_sapiens")))
mresults5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "males only") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(30))

## Females Only
mlist6 <- list(kk2_femHuman2, kk2_femMouse2, kk2_femRat2, kk2_femKillifish2, kk2_femmoth2, kk2_femFly2)
names(mlist6)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
mresults6<-merge_result(mlist6)
mresults6df <- as.data.frame(mresults6) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults6dfb <- mresults6df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults6dfb_subSet <- mresults6dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults6dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults6dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults6dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults6dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults6dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("D_melanogaster", "P_interpunctella", "N_furzeri", "R_norvegicus", "M_musculus", "H_sapiens")))
mresults6dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults6dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults6dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "females only") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(30))

## All
mlist7 <- list(kk2_maleHuman2, kk2_femHuman2, kk2_maleMouse2, kk2_femMouse2, kk2_maleRat2, kk2_femRat2, kk2_maleKillifish2, kk2_femKillifish2, kk2_malemoth2, kk2_femmoth2, kk2_maleFly2, kk2_femFly2)
names(mlist7)<-c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster")
mresults7<-merge_result(mlist7)
# ## I saved all the KEGG Pathways from all the species/sexes in one excel below
# write.xlsx(as.data.frame(mresults7), file = "/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/Results/KEGGpathways(all)11_4_25.xlsx", sheetName = "KEGGall", row.names = F)
# ##
mresults7df <- as.data.frame(mresults7) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults7dfb <- mresults7df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults7dfb_subSet <- mresults7dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults7dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults7dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults7dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults7dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults7dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("f_D.melanogaster", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_N.furzeri", "m_N.furzeri", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_M.musculus", "m_M.musculus", "f_H.sapiens", "m_H.sapiens")))
mresults7dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults7dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults7dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="#DC3220", "suppressed"="#005AB5")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(40))
```

######subset "aging KEGG"
```{r}
agingAtlasKEGGs <- read.csv("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/KEGG/KEGGpathwaysFromAgingAtlas.csv")


mlist7.5 <- list(kk2_maleHuman2, kk2_femHuman2, kk2_maleMouse2, kk2_femMouse2, kk2_maleRat2, kk2_femRat2, kk2_maleKillifish2, kk2_femKillifish2, kk2_malemoth2, kk2_femmoth2, kk2_maleFly2, kk2_femFly2)
names(mlist7.5)<-c("m_H.sapiens", "f_H.sapiens", "m_M.musculus", "f_M.musculus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_N.furzeri", "f_N.furzeri", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_D.melanogaster")
mresults7.5<-merge_result(mlist7.5)
# ##
mresults7.5df <- as.data.frame(mresults7.5) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults7.5dfb <- mresults7.5df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
# mresults7.5dfb_subSet <- mresults7.5dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
#   filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
#   group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
#   slice(1:2)
# mresults7.5dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults7.5dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults7.5dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas <- mresults7.5dfb %>% filter(Description %in% agingAtlasKEGGs$KEGG_Name)
mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas$Cluster <- factor(mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("f_D.melanogaster", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_N.furzeri", "m_N.furzeri", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_M.musculus", "m_M.musculus", "f_H.sapiens", "m_H.sapiens")))
mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas$sign <- as.factor(mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="#DC3220", "suppressed"="#005AB5")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(40))

#length(unique(mresults7.5dfb_agingAtlas$Description)) # 32 , thats how many there are
## The above plots all the aging KEGG pathways that were in my data from the pathways from the Aging Atlas (https://ngdc.cncb.ac.cn/aging/age_related_genes)
```


#### KEGG Interactions (maleOld - maleYoung) - (femaleOld - femaleYoung)
```{r}
## So take a look at one of the KEGG gse results and you will see that in the Description column most KEGG pathways have a species secific indicator by listing the species after a dash. i will need to get rid of these to do a comparison.
kk2_flyInt2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_flyInt) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_humanInt2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_humanInt) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_mouseInt2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_mouseInt) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_ratInt2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_ratInt) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_killifishInt2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_killifishInt) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))
kk2_intMoth2 <- as.data.frame(kk2_intMoth) %>% 
  mutate(Description = gsub("\\ -.*","", Description))

mlist8 <- list(kk2_humanInt2, kk2_mouseInt2, kk2_ratInt2, kk2_killifishInt2, kk2_intMoth2, kk2_flyInt2)
names(mlist8)<-c("H_sapiens", "M_musculus", "R_norvegicus", "N_furzeri", "P_interpunctella", "D_melanogaster")
mresults8<-merge_result(mlist8)
mresults8df <- as.data.frame(mresults8) %>% rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
                               unlist())) / setSize, 
         sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
mresults8dfb <- mresults8df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)
target_strings <- c("activated", "suppressed")
mresults8dfb_subSet <- mresults8dfb %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  filter(sign %in% target_strings) %>%
  group_by(Cluster, sign) %>%
  slice(1:2)
mresults8dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows <- mresults8dfb %>% filter(Description %in% mresults8dfb_subSet$Description) 
mresults8dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster <- factor(mresults8dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$Cluster, 
                             levels = rev(c("D_melanogaster", "P_interpunctella", "N_furzeri", "R_norvegicus", "M_musculus", "H_sapiens")))
mresults8dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign <- as.factor(mresults8dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows$sign)

# got it! Now let's graph
mresults8dfb_subSet_withAdditionalRows %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("activated"="darkred", "suppressed"="forestgreen")) +
  labs(color="NES", title = "Interactions only") +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  #facet_grid(sign~.) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  scale_y_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1), labels=label_wrap(30))

##### Below is old code. You can ignore this for now #########
## now make a list of all the dataframes above 
# mlist14 <- list(kk2_flyInt2, kk2_humanInt2, kk2_mouseInt2, kk2_ratInt2, kk2_killifishInt2, kk2_intMoth2)
# # now name all the dataframes in the list with appropriate species
# names(mlist14)<-c("Human","Mouse","Rat","Killifish","Moth","Fly")
# # now merge all the results/datagframes in the list
# mresults14<-merge_result(mlist14)
# # now make the results a dataframe, calculate a geneRatio, and define the sign
# mresults14df <- as.data.frame(mresults14) %>% rowwise() %>%
#   mutate(geneRatio = (length(strsplit(core_enrichment, "/") %>% 
#                                unlist())) / setSize, 
#          sign = ifelse(NES<0, "suppressed", "activated"))
# # now group the data frame by species, index each row within each group, and arrange by index
# mresults14dfb <- mresults14df %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%  mutate(idx = row_number()) %>% arrange(idx)

# mresults14dfb_subSet <- mresults14dfb %>% slice(1:8)

# # got it! Now let's graph

#   ggplot(aes(x=Cluster,y=Description, color=sign, size=geneRatio)) + 
#   geom_point() +
#   theme_bw() +
#   coord_flip() +
#   #facet_grid(sign~.) +
#   ylab("") +
#   xlab("") +

```


## 1:1 orthologs
```{r}
one2oneOrthologs = read.delim("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/proteomes/primary_transcripts_secondRunDec27/OrthoFinder/Results_Dec27/Orthogroups/Orthogroups_SingleCopyOrthologues.txt", header = F)
one2oneOrthologs$V1 %in% allSigOrthogroups$Orthogroup
one2oneTest <- merge(allSigOrthogroups, one2oneOrthologs, by.x="Orthogroup", by.y="V1") # I don't even know what I am looking at. These are not 1:1 orthologs across all species! It looks like just 1:1 orthologs in any species! Not what I want!
# there are 449 total 1:1 othologs across the 7 species
dim(one2oneTest)
# there are 420 1:1 orthologs that had a significant DEG in one or more of the species


## lets make a dataframe that has the columns of Ortholog, Gene, Sex:Species, log2foldChange
## first let's annotate the Orthogroups to human genes
annot1 <- gene2tx2protein_human %>%
  inner_join(orthogroups) %>% filter(Orthogroup %in% one2oneTest$Orthogroup) %>% 
  select(c("Orthogroup","gene")) %>% full_join(one2oneTest, by="Orthogroup")
#View(annot1)
# there are about 10 undefined orthogroups but the genes are in NCBI. I will manually make a dataframe with Orthogroup and gene and then combine the data frames
annot2 <- data.frame(
  Orthogroup = c("OG0009352","OG0009376","OG0009399","OG0009510","OG0009569","OG0009584","OG0009704","OG0009715","OG0009822","OG0009847"),
  gene = c("CASD1","ASXL2","DPH1","MANF","EMG1","DBT","OXA1L","DCAF13","DIS3L2","SPCS1")
)
one2oneTest2 <- rows_patch(annot1,annot2,by="Orthogroup") # this worked to fill in the NAs

## Now lets try to take the lfc and make it into a single column
#fly
maleFly_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.malefly != "NA", c(1:2,3)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "m_D.melanogaster") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.malefly)
femFly_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.femfly != "NA", c(1:2,4)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "f_D.melanogaster") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.femfly)
#human
maleHuman_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.malehuman != "NA", c(1:2,5)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "m_H.sapiens") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.malehuman)
femHuman_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.femhuman != "NA", c(1:2,6)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "f_H.sapiens") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.femhuman)
#mouse
maleMouse_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.malemouse != "NA", c(1:2,7)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "m_M.musculus") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.malemouse)
femMouse_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.femmouse != "NA", c(1:2,8)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "f_M.musculus") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.femmouse)
#rat
maleRat_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.malerat != "NA", c(1:2,9)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "m_R.norvegicus") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.malerat)
femRat_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.femrat != "NA", c(1:2,10)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "f_R.norvegicus") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.femrat)
#killifish
maleKillifish_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.malekillifish != "NA", c(1:2,11)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "m_N.furzeri") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.malekillifish)
femKillifish_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.femkillifish != "NA", c(1:2,12)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "f_N.furzeri") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.femkillifish)
#moth
maleMoth_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.malemoth != "NA", c(1:2,13)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "m_P.interpunctella") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.malemoth)
femMoth_1to1 <- one2oneTest2[one2oneTest2$medianl2fc.femmoth != "NA", c(1:2,14)] %>% na.omit %>% mutate(Species = "f_P.interpunctella") %>% rename(log2foldChange=medianl2fc.femmoth)
# now lets put them all together with rbind()
rbind(maleFly_1to1, femFly_1to1, 
      maleHuman_1to1, femHuman_1to1, maleMouse_1to1, femMouse_1to1,
      maleRat_1to1, femRat_1to1, maleKillifish_1to1, femKillifish_1to1,
      maleMoth_1to1, femMoth_1to1) -> one2oneAll # this worked well
# Now let's add a column that checks the lfc for up or down reguation of the gene
one2oneAll$sign <- ifelse(one2oneAll$log2foldChange > 0, "upregulated", "downregulated")
# let's set some levels for the columns so they reflect in the graphs
one2oneAll$sign <- factor(one2oneAll$sign, levels = c("upregulated","downregulated"))
# we will order species by phylogeny (insects, fish, mammals)
one2oneAll$Species <- factor(one2oneAll$Species, 
                             levels = rev(c("f_D.melanogaster", "m_D.melanogaster", "f_P.interpunctella", "m_P.interpunctella", "f_N.furzeri", "m_N.furzeri", "f_R.norvegicus", "m_R.norvegicus", "f_M.musculus", "m_M.musculus", "f_H.sapiens", "m_H.sapiens")))

one2oneAll %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=gene, y=Species, color=sign, size=abs(log2foldChange))) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(upregulated="#DC3220", downregulated = "#005AB5")) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  labs(color="", size="|l2fc|", x="1:1 orthologs (annotated to human)") +
  scale_x_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1)) 


## lets finally get the top 20 highest lfc
# first we ordered by the largest absolute value of the lfc (for some reason i had to group the genes or that sorting did not work)
one2oneTop20lfc <- one2oneAll %>% group_by(gene) %>% arrange(desc(abs(log2foldChange)))
# Now lets get the first 20 gene
one2oneTop20lfc <- one2oneTop20lfc %>% ungroup() %>% slice(1:25) # had to go to 25 to get 20 genes as some of the highest lfc were for the same gene from a different sex:species
# now find additional rows if other species also express these genes
one2oneTop20lfc_test <- one2oneAll$gene %in% one2oneTop20lfc$gene
one2oneTop20lfc <- one2oneAll[one2oneTop20lfc_test,]
one2oneTop20lfc %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=gene, y=Species, color=sign, size=abs(log2foldChange))) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(upregulated="#DC3220", downregulated = "#005AB5")) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  labs(color="", size="|l2fc|", x="1:1 orthologs (annotated to human)") +
  annotate("rect", ymin = c(12.5,10.5,8.5,7.5,5.5,3.5,1.5),
           ymax = c(13.5,11.5,9.5,8.5,6.5,4.5,2.5), xmin = 0, xmax = 20.7, fill="red3",
           alpha = .3) +
   annotate("rect", ymin = c(11.5,9.5,8.5,6.5,4.5,2.5,0.5), 
           ymax = c(12.5,10.5,8.5,7.5,5.5,3.5,1.5), xmin = 0, xmax = 20.7, fill="blue", 
           alpha = .3) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1)) # had to change the coloring as Male_Fish no longer on here
```

## 1:1 human aging
```{r}
## here I have downloaded a list of 307 known human aging genes from the Human Genomic Aging Resource at https://genomics.senescence.info/genes/allgenes.php
HGAR_humanAgingGenes = read.delim("/Users/school/Documents/Documents/UAB/UAB005/HAGR_humanAgingGenomicResources/genage_human.csv", header = T, sep = ",")
# I will merge the human aging genes with the all 1:1 orthologs to get a list of known aging genes that have orthologs in other species
HGAR_humanAgingGenes_justSymbol <- HGAR_humanAgingGenes %>%
  select("symbol")
One2One_humanAgingGenes <- merge(one2oneAll, HGAR_humanAgingGenes_justSymbol, by.x="gene", by.y="symbol")
# That left me only about 8 genes (mostly all mammals)
One2One_humanAgingGenes %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=gene, y=Species, color=sign, size=abs(log2foldChange))) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(upregulated="#DC3220", downregulated = "#005AB5")) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust=1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=60, hjust=1, color="black")) +
  labs(color="", size="|l2fc|", x="1:1 orthologs (annotated to human)") +
  scale_x_discrete(guide=guide_axis(n.dodge=1))
```
